sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(100), num_val FLOAT, flag INT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, num_val, flag) VALUES (1, 'hello', 3.14, 0), (2, 'world', NULL, 1), (3, 'test', 2.71, 0), (4, 'fuzzy', 1.0, 1);
SELECT either(NULL, 'no value', value) AS selected_value, num_val FROM test_table WHERE flag = 1;
SELECT either(flag, 'is zero', 'is not zero') AS flag_description, value FROM test_table WHERE value LIKE '%' || either(num_val, '3.14', '2.71') || '%';
SELECT value, either(SUM(num_val), 0, AVG(num_val)) AS aggregated_value FROM test_table GROUP BY value HAVING aggregated_value > 2;
SELECT t1.value, either(t2.flag, 0, 1) AS flag_status FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.value LIKE '%' || t2.value || '%';
SELECT CASE WHEN either(NULL, 0, 1) = 1 THEN 'valid' ELSE 'invalid' END AS validity FROM test_table;
SELECT DISTINCT either(num_val, 0, flag) FROM test_table WHERE value LIKE 'h%';
SELECT 'Boundary check: ' || either(NULL, 'NULL is handled', 'non-null value') AS boundary_result FROM test_table WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table);
